text:
I cannot tell you exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should craft a resume that highlights the technical skills you've acquired, such as networking, cybersecurity, or cloud computing. Then, use professional language to describe your roles and responsibilities during those four years. During the interview, be prepared to answer technical questions with confidence and to demonstrate your knowledge of industry standards and tools. If asked about gaps in your resume, you can frame them as a period spent on personal development or career transition, focusing on your readiness to re-enter the workforce. 

When considering how to present your professional history, it's important to understand the nuances of communication and professional perception. For instance, the way you frame your experience can significantly influence how a hiring manager perceives your potential. For example, focusing on the growth and skills you have gained can often be more effective than dwelling on past challenges. Additionally, being prepared to handle difficult questions with honesty and professionalism can help build trust and credibility throughout your career.
